{'reasoning': 'With the split sequence [Z,X,Y,Z,X,Y,Z,X,Y,Z,X,Y,Z,X,Y,Z], there are 5 X-splits, 5 Y-splits, and 6 Z-splits, so leaves form a uniform 32×32×64 grid. Each corner leaf has exactly 3 face-neighbors (one step inward along X, Y, and Z). Converting those inward-neighbor grid indices back into the 16-bit path (using the given split order) yields the following 24 leaf node IDs.', 'nodes': ['0000000000000100', '0000000000000010', '0000000000000001', '0100100100100000', '0100100100100110', '0100100100100101', '0010010010010110', '0010010010010000', '0010010010010011', '0110110110110010', '0110110110110100', '0110110110110111', '1001001001001101', '1001001001001011', '1001001001001000', '1101101101101001', '1101101101101111', '1101101101101100', '1011011011011111', '1011011011011001', '1011011011011010', '1111111111111011', '1111111111111101', '1111111111111110']}
